title: Public API Data Boundary + Threat Model v0
serialization_rules: Public API serialization must be implemented from this explicit allowlist only.
Public API serialization must not pass through broad project rows.
Public API serialization must not expose raw Supabase rows.
Unknown or non-public records must return RFC 9457-style errors without leaking existence details.
Public API responses must include a request_id when an endpoint exists in a later PR.
PR57 does not create, register, or expose any public API endpoint.
privacy_rules: No email, private founder, admin review, notification, audit, payment, commercial order, or raw import data may appear in public API output.
Pending, submitted, quarantined, rejected, scouted-unreviewed, and admin-review records are not public API records.
Source confidence and Signal Score internals are not public API fields.
Public API output must preserve unknown-value handling and must not infer missing facts.
machine_data_rules: Machine-readable output must be derived from reviewed local snapshots.
Machine-readable output must not crawl external sources at request time.
Machine-readable output must not mutate sitemap, MCP, payment, Signal Score, or source-confidence behavior.
Machine-readable output must not include private source notes or hidden scoring details.
rate_limit_expectations: Any future endpoint must have request bounds before external release.
Any future endpoint must document per-client abuse handling before external release.
Bulk export, scraping support, API keys, and metering are out of scope for PR57.
future_pr58_preconditions: PR58 must depend on this contract.
PR58 must keep a restrictive or disabled-by-default external release posture until human checkpoint approval.
PR58 must enforce allowed_statuses and allowed_public_fields in code.
PR58 must include output validation that blocks denied fields and denied statuses.
PR58 must not expose MCP behavior.
PR58 must not expose payment or commercial order state.
PR58 must not alter sitemap, scoring, source-confidence, or deploy configuration.
